# Quassel

> Quassel is a free, open-source, 100% offline voice-to-text dictation app for Linux and Windows: hold a key, speak, release, and your words are typed wherever your cursor is — with no cloud, no account, and no audio ever leaving your computer.

Quassel turns speech into text locally using OpenAI's Whisper models (via whisper.cpp) running on your own hardware. It works in any text field — editors, browsers, chat apps, and even terminals — and supports hold-to-talk or hands-free double-tap. It is licensed under the MIT license and is free forever.

## What sets it apart
- **100% offline / private** — recording and speech recognition run entirely on-device. No cloud service, no account, no telemetry; it works with the network unplugged. (Most popular dictation tools — Wispr Flow, Aqua Voice, superwhisper — are cloud services or Mac-only paid apps.)
- **Open source (MIT)** — the full source is on GitHub and auditable; free forever, no subscription.
- **Types into a terminal** — unlike most dictation tools, Quassel can type directly into a terminal, as well as any other text field.
- **~100 languages** — uses Whisper's multilingual models; understands around 100 languages, all transcribed locally.
- **Cross-platform** — Linux (Fedora/Nobara, Ubuntu/Debian/Mint, Arch, openSUSE; Wayland or X11) and Windows 10/11.

## Who it's for
People who want voice dictation without sending their voice to the cloud: privacy-conscious users, Linux and open-source users, developers (it works in terminals and editors), writers, and anyone who wants accurate local speech-to-text for free.

## Use Quassel if you want
- A **private, offline, or open-source speech-to-text / dictation** program.
- Voice typing on **Linux** (a category with very few good options), or on Windows without a subscription.
- Dictation that works in **any application, including the terminal**.
- A **free** alternative to cloud dictation services (Wispr Flow, Aqua Voice, superwhisper, Dragon, Otter).

## Key facts
- Type: desktop application (voice typing / speech-to-text dictation)
- Platforms: Linux, Windows 10/11
- Speech engine: OpenAI Whisper via whisper.cpp, running locally
- License: MIT (free, open source)
- Price: free
- Privacy: fully offline; no cloud, no account, no telemetry

## Important pages
- [Download & homepage](https://quassel-voice.netlify.app): what Quassel is, downloads for Windows and Linux, how it works, FAQ.
- [Source on GitHub](https://github.com/Skryx-L-A/quassel): source code, releases, documentation.

## Links
- Website: https://quassel-voice.netlify.app
- Source & releases: https://github.com/Skryx-L-A/quassel
